name: Braised Rabbit in Reisling with Tomatoes, Rapini and Creme
recipeIngredient: 12 red teardrop or cherry tomatoes, cut into halves
recipeInstructions:
Marinating the rabbit:
1. Butcher the rabbit into 2 legs, 2 forearms, and 2 loins.
2. Combine the marinade ingredients together (shallots, Dijon mustard, Reisling wine, bay leaves, and basil) in a bowl.
3. Add the rabbit pieces to the marinade. Marinate overnight. Save the bones for a stock.
Braising the rabbit:
1. Heat oil in a large frying pan. Drain the rabbit pieces (legs and forearms) and set them aside.
2. Season the legs and forearms with salt and pepper, then sprinkle with flour.
3. Sear each side until browned, then add them to a braising pan.
4. Wipe out the frying pan, add more oil, and cook the vegetables (carrot, celery, and leek) until golden.
5. Deglaze with Reisling wine, then add the vegetables and wine to the rabbit.
6. Add the remaining marinade, tomatoes, pigs foot, and enough stock to come halfway up the meat.
7. Braise for 30-40 minutes or until the legs are tender. Remove the legs and set aside.
8. Strain the braising juices into a bowl.
9. Sear the loin in a frying pan over high heat, then slice on the diagonal into 1-inch pieces.
10. Return the legs to the braising juices and reheat them briefly.
11. Add olive oil to the frying pan, then sear the rapini until just cooked through, about 10 minutes.
12. Add the tomatoes and garlic, cooking for an additional 5 minutes.
13. Serve by arranging a foreleg and back leg on each of 4 warm plates. Top with the loin pieces, rapini, and tomatoes.
14. Pour the sauce over the meat and finish with a spoonful of crème fraîche.
15. Roasted red bliss potatoes are an optional accompaniment.
